steps.title: Match population to density
steps.content: The claim says a very populous country can be less densely populated than a much smaller country. The example must compare population size and population density across two countries.

steps.title: Reject incomplete comparisons
steps.content:
• Choice A discusses only Angola's size and population.
  • Choice C compares countries with similar density, not the claimed contrast.
  • Choice D compares years, not population with density.

steps.title: Use the density contrast
steps.content: The remaining choice compares high-population, low-density Angola with lower-population, higher-density Sao Tome and Principe, directly illustrating the claim. The answer is B.
